title: 1.1 Reports whether write operations succeed or fail when a drive becomes unavailable (e.g., upload completing with 3/4 drives, or write failing due to insufficient quorum)
1.2: Reports the specific error code returned when write quorum fails (e.g., SlowDownWrite with HTTP 503)
1.3: Reports whether files stored before drive failure can still be read (e.g., checksum verification showing data integrity)
1.4: Reports the specific error code returned when read quorum fails (e.g., SlowDownRead with HTTP 503)
1.5: Explains what triggers healing when a drive comes back online (e.g., missing format.json, stale data, healing tracker present)
1.6: Explains criteria for determining if an object needs healing (e.g., errFileNotFound, errFileCorrupt, metadata mismatch)
1.7: Reports log messages during healing operations (e.g., Healing drive X', Healing of drive X is finished)
1.8: Identifies the metric names for drive availability (e.g., drives_online_count, drives_offline_count)
1.9: Reports metric values before and after drive failure (e.g., drives_online changing from 4 to 3)
1.10: Reports cluster health status changes when drives go offline (e.g., via Prometheus metrics showing online/offline drive counts)
